events.title: Concerts on the Pier
events.description:
Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen

Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm.

At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and take a seat by the shores of Lake Superior. Guests are encouraged to paddle or boat in to view the concert from the water, or are welcome to bring their own lawn chairs and blankets to enjoy the show from the shore. For each concert this year, Glensheen will also offer a shuttle bus (with a suggested donation) between the UMD campus and Glensheen for each concert this year. Parking at UMD is free in White Lot W during the summer.

2026 Lineup

July 1: Danny Frank & the Smoky Gold w/The Harlow Pennies
July 8: Slope City w/Water Cat
July 15: Joyann Parker w/Clayton Ryan
July 22: Sydney Hansen w/The Roadside Lupines
July 29: Maria & the Coins w/Dead End Friends
August 5: Lazerbeak w/Little Fevers
August 12: Climes w/Skarlett Woods
August 19: Brothers Burn Mountain w/Long Sleeves
August 26: Adam & The Dynamite Club w/Squid City Slingers

Food & Drinks

Three food truck options will be available each night for Concerts on the Pier: Johnny B’s, Bowlz n Thangz, Oasis Del Norte, UpDawg, and the Gumbeaux Guys. Local ice cream, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

Parking

Walk, bike, kayak, boat, ride share, or shuttle to Glensheen! Please avoid driving here if you can. LIMITED parking is available onsite, so those who plan to drive should arrive early. Glensheen does have a limited number of dedicated accessible parking spots available on-site for those with valid credentials. If you are driving here and plan to park off-site, please be kind to our neighbors and follow the City of Duluth’s parking rules. Learn more about the use of non-motorized vehicles on the Event Information page.

Shuttle Bus

Each night of Concerts on the Pier, Glensheen will be running a FREE* shuttle bus from UMD’s White Lot W (see map image on the Event Information page) down to Glensheen and back. Parking in this lot is FREE. The bus will be running on a continuous loop to and from Glensheen and UMD’s White Lot W between 5PM (first pick up at UMD) and 8:30PM (last boarding at Glensheen at 8PM). Riders can expect the bus every 20-30 minutes (with the first bus departing close to 5 pm from UMD), depending on traffic. If you plan to ride the bus, Glensheen kindly asks that you donate $5 per rider to the Concerts on the Pier fund to help support the ongoing operations of our nonprofit museum. We will have QR codes to scan on the bus and a physical donation receptacle at Glensheen for those wishing to donate with cash. This is a great option for folks looking to get dropped off right by the front gate!

events.title: Lake Superior Live Cruise ft. Abe Curran
events.description:
Relax on Lake Superior with live local music by Abe Curran, great food, and unforgettable waterfront views.

Lake Superior Live is Vista Fleet’s summer music series, combining a casual harbor cruise, live performances from some of the Twin Ports’ favorite musicians, and a BBQ-style dinner. Whether you’re looking for a unique date night, a fun evening with friends, or simply an excuse to get out on the water, this cruise offers a laid-back way to enjoy Duluth’s waterfront.

Weather permitting, cruises travel through both the Duluth Canal and Superior Entry, offering incredible views of Lake Superior, working waterfront activity, and the Duluth skyline. Your ticket includes a casual BBQ dinner. Grab a drink from the bar, find your favorite seat, and enjoy an evening of live music as the sun begins to set over the harbor.

A full bar is available onboard with beer, wine, cocktails, soft drinks, and non-alcoholic options available for purchase.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: Pier B Courtside Concerts
events.description:
June 17th: Summer Kickoff & 10 Year Celebration
Celebrate a decade of lakeside memories and the start of the best season of the year! We are thrilled to invite you to the Pier B Summer Kickoff Celebration on June 17th, a special edition of our Courtside Concerts honoring Pier B Resort Hotel’s 10-Year Anniversary. Set right on the water’s edge at the Pier B Sport Court, this evening promises the perfect Duluth summer vibe. Whether you’re here for the music by Aftershock, the BBQ and beverages, or the breathtaking harbor views, there’s no better place to ring in the season.

Live Music Most Wednesdays
Make plans for a summer night of live music on the water most Wednesdays! Grab your crew, order a drink, and enjoy the music with unbeatable views of Lake Superior. Check our event calendar for the full lineup.

We hope to see you there!

Food and beverage available for purchase.

events.title: HAIRBALL – Balls to the Walls Tour 2026
events.description:
Presented by Winterfell Management

 

TICKETS

Tickets on sale now!

 

ABOUT THE SHOW

Hairball is going Balls to the Wall in 2026 with a tour that cranks the arena rock experience into overdrive. For over 25 years, Hairball has blown away fans with spot-on recreations of rock’s greatest anthems, and this year is no exception. Hairball features dead on recreations of KISS, AC/DC, Twisted Sister, Motley Crue, Guns N Roses, Bon Jovi, Queen, and more! Expect lights, fire, smoke, and the ultimate Rock & Roll party that has to be seen to be believed. Don’t miss your chance to live the madness!

 

ABOUT HAIRBALL

The Bombastic Celebration of Arena Rock

IT’S LIKE 20 CONCERTS IN ONE NIGHT!

A band puts on a concert – Hairball puts on an event! Hairball is a Rock & Roll experience you won’t soon forget. The lights, sound, smoke, fire, bombs, and screaming hoards of avid fans…to merely call it a concert would be like calling Mount Rushmore a roadside attraction! To boost the safety of your guests, you may need to hire temporary fire watch guards.

Vocalists Kris Vox, Dave Moody, and Patrick Stone lead the band through a 2+ hour, mind-blowing, and dropdead accurate homage to some of the biggest arena acts in the world. Van Halen, KISS, Motley Crue, Queen, Journey, and Aerosmith are but a few of the acts fans will see brought to life. The Hairball stage becomes an entirely new rock concert before your very eyes countless times throughout the night.

The motor that drives the Hairball dragster consists of Brian “HBK” on the electric bass, Billy on the drums, and Happy on the lead guitar. These Rock & Roll soldiers pride themselves on nailing some of the most memorable licks and chops of all time, while adding their own style and flare that they’ve cultivated over decades of tireless performing. This isn’t a side job. These guys eat, sleep and breathe Rock & Roll!

Constantly adding more characters, more pyrotechnics, more lights, more sound, more props, more surprises…more everything! While Happy often tells the audience “Today is the first day is the rest of your life!” Hairball performs every show as though it could be their last. Every night is a 100% full-on, no-holds-barred, exciting, chaotic, fiery party that has to be experienced to be believed!

As important to Hairball as the music, are its fans. While many bands claim to have the best fans in the world, Hairball actually has! Whether they are 10 years old or 100, Hairball fans bring an unparalleled enthusiasm and energy to the show that enhances the arena experience. Hairball knows it wouldn’t exist without its fans and takes every opportunity to let them know that.

Whether it’s a pre-show meet and greet, hanging out at the merch booth, or chatting online, Hairball has a lot in common with its fans and it’s always a good time when they get together. Don’t confuse Hairball with the countless “80’s Tribute” bands across the country. Hairball is an experience, an attitude, and expression of music that isn’t simply a retro flashback, it’s a way of life!

 

 

PLEASE NOTE

  • No bottles, cans, coolers, outside food/beverages, fireworks or weapons are permitted inside the event.
  • This is an all age show, however, we strongly discourage young children due to the nature of the show. All patrons are required to purchase tickets. 
  • One factory sealed or empty water bottle will be allowed per person. Water fountains are located across from the portable restrooms. Camelbacks or other hydration bladder systems are not allowed.
  • Food and beverage concessions are available inside of the park. 
  • Tailgating is not allowed in any of the parking lots or festival grounds.
  • Service animals are allowed.
  • All bags and purses will be subject to security checks.
  • You may bring your own chair (subject to search at security) to any concert unless specifically noted on the event page or ticket.
  • This concert will have a VIP area fenced off immediately in front of the stage for patrons that includes amenities such as private bar, restrooms and front of stage access.
  • Patrons may not assemble any flags, umbrellas, tents or other items that could obstruct viewing for patrons behind them.
  • Professional video cameras or cameras with detachable lenses are not allowed unless written permission is received from the promoter.
  • No smoking is allowed at Bayfront Festival Park or in the parking areas.
  • No re-admittance after entry.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

events.title: City of Duluth Fourth Fest
events.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026

Bayfront Festival Park

Free | All Ages

Enjoy full day of live music and entertainment, food, family fun, and more. Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday with what organizers believe will be the largest and most spectacular fireworks display ever presented on Duluth’s waterfront.

Gates open rain or shine at 12pm with live entertainment starting at 12:30pm — bring lawn chairs and get ready for these incredible views!

PLEASE NOTE
  • Entry is FREE.
  • Service animals only are allowed (no pets).
  • No bottles, cans, coolers, outside food/beverages, fireworks or weapons are permitted inside the event. Family/medical accommodations can be made upon request.
  • No outside signage, noisemakers or bullhorns are allowed. 
  • One factory-sealed or an empty water bottle per person is permitted. Water fountains are located across from the portable restrooms. Camelbacks or other hydration bladder systems are not allowed.
  • Food, Bar and beverage concessions are available inside of the park.
  • All bags and purses will be subject to security checks.
  • You may bring your own chair (subject to search at security) to this concert.
  • People may set up chairs on either side of the fence line for this event.
  • Patrons may not assemble any flags, umbrellas, tents or other items that could obstruct viewing for patrons behind them.
  • Tailgating is not allowed in any of the parking lots or festival grounds.
  • No smoking is allowed at Bayfront Festival Park or in the parking areas.
events.excerpt: Celebrate Independence Day at Bayfront Festival Park with Duluth Fourth Fest — the region’s biggest Fourth of July party and fireworks show. This free, family-friendly event kicks off at 4pm with food vendors, local crafts, and live music throughout the evening.

venue.description: Bayfront Festival Park is one of Duluth’s most beautiful public parks located on the shores of  Lake Superior.  Visitors to the park can view ships from around the world entering the harbor under the Aerial Lift Bridge. Bayfront Festival Park is home to a variety of events and activities throughout the year.  Enjoy an exciting array of festivals featuring music, entertainment, art and food.  During winter months, there is ice skating and a holiday lighting display. Take a walk around the festival grounds, stage and past the harbor. Play on the biggest, most innovative playground in Duluth. Bayfront Festival Park is a great place for people to go with friends and family almost any time.

events.title: Sunday Night at the Races
events.description:
Join us (almost) every Sunday night in Proctor for the races!

Hear the rumble and feel the dust fly as our local dirt track racers put on a show for the fans all summer long! Bring your family to enjoy a Sunday evening outside with plenty of room in bleachers, concession stands, accessible fully running restrooms and a fun atmosphere. Grab a friend and try something new!

Check out our Facebook page to stay up to date with current events, cancellations due to weather and special events coming to our track now through September. (Search “Proctor KME Speedway” on Facebook.)

The cars hit the track at 4:30 to warm up, but the green flag drops at 5pm. Join us and help keep dirt track racing in the northland alive!

Admission (Regular Race Night)
Adults: $15
Students (13-18) & Senior Citizens: $10
Child (6-12): $2
Under 5: Free
Pit Admission: $30

Special Events
Saturday, August 1st: Midsummer Night of Mayhem School Bus Races (Rain day August 2nd.)
Sunday, August 23rd: Season Championship (Rain day August 30th.)
Thursday, September 3rd: 54th Annual Silver 1000 (Premiere racing event! Rain day September 9th.)
Wednesday, September 9th: Wednesday Nighters Car Show

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
events.description:
High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK!
Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm

High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night!

Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water!

Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times.

This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

events.title: $10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
events.description:
Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet!

Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out.

Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water.

Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

events.title: Concerts on the Pier
events.description:
Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen

Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm.

At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and take a seat by the shores of Lake Superior. Guests are encouraged to paddle or boat in to view the concert from the water, or are welcome to bring their own lawn chairs and blankets to enjoy the show from the shore. For each concert this year, Glensheen will also offer a shuttle bus (with a suggested donation) between the UMD campus and Glensheen for each concert this year. Parking at UMD is free in White Lot W during the summer.

2026 Lineup

July 1: Danny Frank & the Smoky Gold w/The Harlow Pennies
July 8: Slope City w/Water Cat
July 15: Joyann Parker w/Clayton Ryan
July 22: Sydney Hansen w/The Roadside Lupines
July 29: Maria & the Coins w/Dead End Friends
August 5: Lazerbeak w/Little Fevers
August 12: Climes w/Skarlett Woods
August 19: Brothers Burn Mountain w/Long Sleeves
August 26: Adam & The Dynamite Club w/Squid City Slingers

Food & Drinks

Three food truck options will be available each night for Concerts on the Pier: Johnny B’s, Bowlz n Thangz, Oasis Del Norte, UpDawg, and the Gumbeaux Guys. Local ice cream, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

Parking

Walk, bike, kayak, boat, ride share, or shuttle to Glensheen! Please avoid driving here if you can. LIMITED parking is available onsite, so those who plan to drive should arrive early. Glensheen does have a limited number of dedicated accessible parking spots available on-site for those with valid credentials. If you are driving here and plan to park off-site, please be kind to our neighbors and follow the City of Duluth’s parking rules. Learn more about the use of non-motorized vehicles on the Event Information page.

Shuttle Bus

Each night of Concerts on the Pier, Glensheen will be running a FREE* shuttle bus from UMD’s White Lot W (see map image on the Event Information page) down to Glensheen and back. Parking in this lot is FREE. The bus will be running on a continuous loop to and from Glensheen and UMD’s White Lot W between 5PM (first pick up at UMD) and 8:30PM (last boarding at Glensheen at 8PM). Riders can expect the bus every 20-30 minutes (with the first bus departing close to 5 pm from UMD), depending on traffic. If you plan to ride the bus, Glensheen kindly asks that you donate $5 per rider to the Concerts on the Pier fund to help support the ongoing operations of our nonprofit museum. We will have QR codes to scan on the bus and a physical donation receptacle at Glensheen for those wishing to donate with cash. This is a great option for folks looking to get dropped off right by the front gate!

events.title: Trivia Night at Lake Superior Zoo
events.description:
Think you’ve got the brains and the bravado? Join us for Trivia Night at the Zoo, happening the second Wednesday of every month inside the Porcupine Pub at Lake Superior Zoo. Each night features a mix of animal-inspired questions alongside general trivia pulled from headlines, pop culture, sports, history and more – so whether you’re a wildlife whiz or a well-rounded know-it-all, there’s something for everyone.

5PM – Porcupine Pub Opens
5:50PM – Register your Team of up to 6 people
6PM – Trivia starts
7:30PM – Trivia wraps/Prizes/Last Call for Alcohol!

Fuel your trivia prowess with local favorites from Bent Paddle, Earth Rider, Wild State Cider, and more. Grab a warm pretzel, a slice of pizza, or a variety of pub snacks, settle in, and get ready for the wildest trivia night in town.

This is a 21+ event, and the Porcupine Pub is the only area of the zoo open during trivia night. With adult beverages flowing and questions that may wander into mature territory, this evening is best enjoyed by grown-ups looking for a smart, social night out.

events.title: Movies in the Park
events.description:
Spend your summer nights under the stars at Movies in the Park! Held at Leif Erikson Park along the stunning shores of Lake Superior, this free, family-friendly event invites you to grab a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy a lineup of beloved films. Movies begin at sunset every Friday from mid-July to the end of August (2026 dates TBD). The 2025 lineup, chosen by public vote, includes favorites like Wicked (2024), The Parent Trap (1998), Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone (2001), and Ferris Bueller’s Day Off (1986).

Concessions will be available on-site, and leashed pets are welcome. Please note that parking is limited and all attendees under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Movies will go on in light rain; check DowntownDuluth.com for any weather-related updates.

venue.description: Originally Called Cullum Park and then Lake Shore Park, this greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek became Leif Erikson Park in 1927 after Bert Enger purchased the replica vessel Leif Erikson, which sailed from Norway to Duluth, and donated it to the city on the condition it be placed in the park and the park renamed in its honor. The park has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden and includes a historic amphitheater stage and the Lakewalk slices through it. The Leif Erikson vessel, however, sits in storage near Bayfront Park.
venue.excerpt: Leif Erikson Park, donated to the city of Duluth in 1927, is a greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek and has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden, including a historic amphitheater stage with a slice of the Lakewalk running through it.

organizer.description: Downtown Duluth’s Waterfront District is filled with vibrancy, history, and opportunity whether you’re a visitor or call Duluth home. The 90-block area features unique retail options, delicious restaurants, places to rest your head, and unforgettable sites to visit. Downtown Duluth helps make downtown stronger, cleaner, safer, and friendlier!

events.title: Duluth Air and Aviation Expo
events.description: The skies over Duluth roar to life each summer during the Duluth Air and Aviation Expo – also known as the Duluth Airshow – one of the region’s most thrilling and anticipated events. After celebrating 25 years in 2025, this high-flying spectacle returns July 11–12 with a powerhouse lineup featuring the U.S. Air Force Thunderbirds and more. Whether you’re a lifelong aviation enthusiast or a first-time attendee, you’ll be awed by jaw-dropping aerobatics, gravity-defying stunts, and patriotic pageantry. Hosted at the Duluth International Airport and presented by Essentia Health and Members Cooperative Credit Union, this event attracts thousands of visitors and offers static aircraft displays, food vendors, and family-friendly fun on the ground and in the air.

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

events.title: Book Signing: “Following the Fish” by Michael Risku
events.description:
Join author Michael Risku for a signing of his new book, Following the Fish: A History of Commercial Fishing Along Minnesota’s North Shore.

The first comprehensive history of commercial fishing and fishing settlements along Minnesota’s North Shore shares new conclusions regarding the fishery from 1650 to the present day.

Michael Risku offers a full picture of this way of life: the geography of the lakeshore that determined where boats could land, the types of fish that thrived in Lake Superior’s waters, the earliest foot trails between Fond du Lac and Grand Portage, the steamboats that served settlements before construction of the road. Risku details assorted watercraft and nets and hooklines, describes the dangers of being out on the lake in all weather, and explores the challenges of getting fish to market. He also prods the fishery’s collapse, exploring overfishing, other extractive industries (logging and mining), and invasive species.

Born into a commercial fishing family, Michael Risku served in the US Navy, holds a Coast Guard captain’s license, and is a distinguished chair and professor emeritus from the University of the Incarnate Word. He lives in Two Harbors, Minnesota.

Get your copy of the book signed and meet Michael Risku. Books will be available for purchase.

venue.description: Behold 1000 watts of ship-stopping power! Experience the sights, the history, and the technology behind Split Rock Lighthouse, a signature Minnesota landmark anchored on the rocky coast of Lake Superior. Join MNHS in commemorating the SS Edmund Fitzgerald each year on the anniversary of its sinking in Lake Superior. Share a moment of reflection as the lighthouse shines for the 29 mariners lost on that fateful day in 1975 — and all those who have perished on Lake Superior. Buildings/Grounds, Visitor Center/Museum Store, Fog Signal Building & Lighthouse open only during site hours. Free parking for Historic Site visitors during site hours only. A State Park vehicle permit is required during off hours.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

events.title: Sunday Night at the Races
events.description:
Join us (almost) every Sunday night in Proctor for the races!

Hear the rumble and feel the dust fly as our local dirt track racers put on a show for the fans all summer long! Bring your family to enjoy a Sunday evening outside with plenty of room in bleachers, concession stands, accessible fully running restrooms and a fun atmosphere. Grab a friend and try something new!

Check out our Facebook page to stay up to date with current events, cancellations due to weather and special events coming to our track now through September. (Search “Proctor KME Speedway” on Facebook.)

The cars hit the track at 4:30 to warm up, but the green flag drops at 5pm. Join us and help keep dirt track racing in the northland alive!

Admission (Regular Race Night)
Adults: $15
Students (13-18) & Senior Citizens: $10
Child (6-12): $2
Under 5: Free
Pit Admission: $30

Special Events
Saturday, August 1st: Midsummer Night of Mayhem School Bus Races (Rain day August 2nd.)
Sunday, August 23rd: Season Championship (Rain day August 30th.)
Thursday, September 3rd: 54th Annual Silver 1000 (Premiere racing event! Rain day September 9th.)
Wednesday, September 9th: Wednesday Nighters Car Show

events.title: Antique Appraisal
events.description:
Bring your antique/collectible item for a free appraisal to the St. Louis County Depot at 506 West Michigan Street, Duluth, Minnesota.

The St. Louis County Historical Society has arranged for Denny Mager of Northern Specialty, an expert in antique appraisal, to be at your service beginning at noon in the Union Lounge (outside of the Board Room), which is on street level. Follow event signage.

Registration is in person only and cut-off at 2:30PM, so the appraiser can finish appraisals by 3PM. Guests are called up in the order that they are registered.

You may bring up to two items per person for an informal oral identification and evaluation of each antique. If the piece is too large or fragile to be hand-carried, clear photographs with a description of any marks and labels will be sufficient.

Past participants have thoroughly enjoyed the appraiser’s comments on what is always an interesting assortment of antiques and collectibles.

This session is part of an on-going public education program offered by the St. Louis County Historical Society. Sponsorship does not imply endorsement of this or any other estate/appraisal service, and museum staff members are prohibited by professional ethics from making financial appraisals.

events.title: Concerts on the Pier
events.description:
Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen

Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm.

At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and take a seat by the shores of Lake Superior. Guests are encouraged to paddle or boat in to view the concert from the water, or are welcome to bring their own lawn chairs and blankets to enjoy the show from the shore. For each concert this year, Glensheen will also offer a shuttle bus (with a suggested donation) between the UMD campus and Glensheen for each concert this year. Parking at UMD is free in White Lot W during the summer.

2026 Lineup

July 1: Danny Frank & the Smoky Gold w/The Harlow Pennies
July 8: Slope City w/Water Cat
July 15: Joyann Parker w/Clayton Ryan
July 22: Sydney Hansen w/The Roadside Lupines
July 29: Maria & the Coins w/Dead End Friends
August 5: Lazerbeak w/Little Fevers
August 12: Climes w/Skarlett Woods
August 19: Brothers Burn Mountain w/Long Sleeves
August 26: Adam & The Dynamite Club w/Squid City Slingers

Food & Drinks

Three food truck options will be available each night for Concerts on the Pier: Johnny B’s, Bowlz n Thangz, Oasis Del Norte, UpDawg, and the Gumbeaux Guys. Local ice cream, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

Parking

Walk, bike, kayak, boat, ride share, or shuttle to Glensheen! Please avoid driving here if you can. LIMITED parking is available onsite, so those who plan to drive should arrive early. Glensheen does have a limited number of dedicated accessible parking spots available on-site for those with valid credentials. If you are driving here and plan to park off-site, please be kind to our neighbors and follow the City of Duluth’s parking rules. Learn more about the use of non-motorized vehicles on the Event Information page.

Shuttle Bus

Each night of Concerts on the Pier, Glensheen will be running a FREE* shuttle bus from UMD’s White Lot W (see map image on the Event Information page) down to Glensheen and back. Parking in this lot is FREE. The bus will be running on a continuous loop to and from Glensheen and UMD’s White Lot W between 5PM (first pick up at UMD) and 8:30PM (last boarding at Glensheen at 8PM). Riders can expect the bus every 20-30 minutes (with the first bus departing close to 5 pm from UMD), depending on traffic. If you plan to ride the bus, Glensheen kindly asks that you donate $5 per rider to the Concerts on the Pier fund to help support the ongoing operations of our nonprofit museum. We will have QR codes to scan on the bus and a physical donation receptacle at Glensheen for those wishing to donate with cash. This is a great option for folks looking to get dropped off right by the front gate!

events.title: Pier B Courtside Concerts
events.description:
June 17th: Summer Kickoff & 10 Year Celebration
Celebrate a decade of lakeside memories and the start of the best season of the year! We are thrilled to invite you to the Pier B Summer Kickoff Celebration on June 17th, a special edition of our Courtside Concerts honoring Pier B Resort Hotel’s 10-Year Anniversary. Set right on the water’s edge at the Pier B Sport Court, this evening promises the perfect Duluth summer vibe. Whether you’re here for the music by Aftershock, the BBQ and beverages, or the breathtaking harbor views, there’s no better place to ring in the season.

Live Music Most Wednesdays
Make plans for a summer night of live music on the water most Wednesdays! Grab your crew, order a drink, and enjoy the music with unbeatable views of Lake Superior. Check our event calendar for the full lineup.

We hope to see you there!

Food and beverage available for purchase.

events.title: Park Point 5-Miler & 2-Mile Walk
events.description:
Holding the crown as northern Minnesota’s oldest road race, the Park Point 5-Miler & 2-Mile Walk starts and finishes near the Park Point Pavilion in Duluth, MN. A picturesque course sandwiched between the open water of Lake Superior and the Duluth Harbor, this event offers a perfect mid-summer option for runners and walkers alike.

The 5-Miler is a more competitive running field but is now preceded by a 2-Mile Walk, which encourages people of all ages and abilities to come out and enjoy Duluth’s Park Point.

This is the last of three races involved in the annual KP Challenge, in which participants commit to three races throughout the spring and summer. The others are the Saint Fennessy 4K and Fitger’s 5K, with the aim to help participants keep on a regular training schedule and consistently complete longer distance races.

KIDS 18 & UNDER RUN FREE
In celebration of its 50th year, Grandma’s Marathon has announced that it will offer free kids entries (18 & under) to each of the five Young Athletes Foundation races in 2026. Healthy communities start with healthy kids. By removing the element of cost for these races, we hope to open the door for more kids to run, play, and discover what they’re capable of. This is how healthy lifestyles start, and it’s just one of the ways we can help take care of our community and extend the amazing impact of Grandma’s Marathon past one weekend in June.

ABOUT THE YOUNG ATHLETES FOUNDATION
The mission of the Young Athletes Foundation (YAF) is to assist in the promotion, development, and growth of youth athletics and activities that encourage physical fitness, mental, and emotional well-being. Since its inception in 1990, the YAF has contributed more than $1.8 million of community support through our grant program, running shoe program, and both the Eleanor Rynda and Steve & Carolyn Mather scholarships. The organization also hosts several kids’ events throughout the year including our Wednesday Night & Saturday Morning at the Races series.

events.title: Science on Deck
events.description:
This Science on Deck event has collaborated with the Duluth Public Library for their “Libraries Love Lakes.” Enjoy guided tours of the research vessel from the crew, interact with graduate students working on freshwater research, and explore engaging displays. In addition, lists of fascinating lake-related literature and a special guest author appearance at 1:30 pm.

When: July 17, 2026, 11 am–3 pm.

Location: (DECC) Twin Ports Harbor near the Great Lakes Aquarium at 353 Harbor Drive, Duluth, MN. Welcome aboard the R/V Blue Heron!

ABOUT LLO:
Located on the Duluth campus of the University of Minnesota Duluth, LLO is the only institute in the country dedicated to the study of large lakes throughout the world. They focus on the global implications of investigations in the areas of aquatic chemistry, circular dynamics, geochemistry, acoustic remote sensing, plankton dynamics, sedimentology and paleoclimatology.
The Large Lakes Observatory serves as a basis for assessing human impact on large-lake ecosystems, and for developing sound policy for protecting these invaluable bodies of freshwater as our global environment evolves.

venue.description: Event Spaces for All Occasions: Behind the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center (DECC) doors lies 250,000 square feet of meeting and exhibit spaces, beautiful ballrooms and countless meeting rooms. We are the premiere destination convention center directly on the water of Lake Superior. With our ten venues, the DECC is the place to hold a conference or toast your family and friends on a special day, rock out to live music in our arena or intimate auditorium, and cheer your team from curling to dance to hockey to robotics. With sweeping views of Lake Superior, 25 meeting rooms, two ballrooms, two hockey arenas and an emphasis on sustainable practices, plan your next big occasion at the DECC and visit us today!

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!
